data.description:
## What
G5 (decomposition) currently judges from ticket TEXT and false-flagged an epic that already had 6
children as a "flat, undecomposed list". The store already loads the real children — `ctx.children`
is populated by the N+1 read in `orchestrator.py:166-182` and counted in `pass1.py:427`. Make that
fact authoritative for G5 so the model never counts children itself.

## Scope / files
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/pass1.py` — inject an authoritative `DECOMPOSITION STATE (from store):
  N direct children: [alias - title (status)]` block into the G5 finder context from `ctx.children`.
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py` (or the DET-invariant seam) — add a deterministic veto that
  drops any G5 "flat/undecomposed" finding when `len(ctx.children) > 0`.
- `tests/unit/` — regression test for the previously-misfired case.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The G5 finder context includes the store-sourced authoritative child summary.
- [ ] A DET guard suppresses a G5 decomposition finding when the ticket has >=1 child (count from the
      store, not the model); G5 still judges child-content/altitude.
- [ ] Regression test: an epic with children no longer yields a G5 "undecomposed" finding; a genuinely
      monolithic childless ticket still does.
data.title: Deterministic G5 decomposition signal (store-derived child count feeds the finder + DET veto)
